description: tty-theme — AI terminal theme generator. Full implementation with local-first GCP emulation: Firebase Emulator Suite (Firestore, Auth, Hosting) + Docker Compose replaces all gcloud/firebase deploy commands. No real GCP infra touched during development. Terraform IaC for all GCP resources (validate/plan locally, apply for production).

userStories.title: Repo scaffold and pyproject.toml
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a clean project scaffold with all dependencies declared so I can start building immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml exists with project name 'tty-theme', Python >=3.11, and all Phase 0-3 deps: typer, pillow, scikit-learn, imagehash, sentence-transformers, keyring, httpx
uv.lock is generated and committed
Directory structure exists: cli/, modes/, generator/serializers/, image/, cache/, providers/, security/, themes/, schema/, tests/
Each directory has an __init__.py
pyproject.toml [tool.ruff] section enables S rules (bandit), E, F, I
ruff check passes

userStories.title: SQLite schema — themes, cost_log, audit_log tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SQLite schema initialized so the cache and cost tracking can persist data.
acceptanceCriteria: cache/db.py implements a ThemeRepository class with init_db(), save_theme(), get_by_hash(), list_themes() methods
init_db() creates themes table with columns: id, query_hash, query_raw, input_type, name, theme_data, embedding, source, provider, cost_usd, created_at
init_db() creates cost_log table with columns: id, date, provider, calls, cost_usd
init_db() creates audit_log table with columns: id, ip_hash, query_hash, input_type, provider, tier_used, cost_usd, status, created_at
Index on themes.query_hash is created
DB file defaults to ~/.local/share/tty-theme/cache.db (created if not exists)
Tests pass: init_db idempotent, save and retrieve a theme by hash
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Security module stubs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need stub implementations of the security modules so the import graph is complete before building the real logic.
acceptanceCriteria: security/input_sanitizer.py: sanitize_prompt(text: str) -> str — stub returns text unchanged, raises ValueError if len > 200
security/ssrf_guard.py: check_url(url: str) -> None — stub raises ValueError for non-https URLs
security/keystore.py: get_key(provider: str) -> str | None and set_key(provider: str, key: str) -> None stubs
security/rate_limiter.py: RateLimiter class stub with check() -> bool always returning True
All files importable with no runtime errors
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Ghostty ThemeSerializer base class and Ghostty serializer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ThemeSerializer abstract base class and the Ghostty key=value serializer so themes can be output in Ghostty format.
acceptanceCriteria: generator/serializers/base.py: abstract ThemeSerializer with serialize(palette: dict) -> str and file_extension() -> str abstract methods
generator/serializers/ghostty.py: GhosttySerializer implements ThemeSerializer
GhosttySerializer.serialize() outputs all 16 palette entries (palette = 0..15) plus background, foreground, cursor-color, selection-background, selection-foreground
Output format: one key=value per line, hex values as #RRGGBB
serialize() raises ValueError if any of the 21 required keys is missing or any hex value is invalid
Tests pass: round-trip a full 21-key palette through GhosttySerializer
ruff check passes

userStories.title: iTerm2 serializer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the iTerm2 XML plist serializer so themes can be exported as .itermcolors files.
acceptanceCriteria: generator/serializers/iterm2.py: ITerm2Serializer implements ThemeSerializer
file_extension() returns '.itermcolors'
serialize() outputs valid XML plist with Ansi 0-15 Color, Background Color, Foreground Color, Bold Color, Cursor Color, Cursor Text Color, Selection Color, Selected Text Color, Link Color
Each color dict has Alpha Component=1, Color Space=sRGB, Red/Green/Blue Component as float (hex / 255.0, 6 decimal places)
Bold Color maps to palette 15 (bright white); Link Color maps to palette 4 (blue)
Tests pass: round-trip #1a1a2e through serializer produces Red=0.101961, Green=0.101961, Blue=0.180392
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Theme validator — schema and WCAG contrast
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the theme validator to reject LLM output that is missing keys or has insufficient contrast.
acceptanceCriteria: generator/validator.py: validate_theme(theme_str: str) -> dict parses a Ghostty key=value string
Raises ValueError if any of the 21 required keys is missing
Raises ValueError if any value is not a valid #RRGGBB hex
Raises ValueError if WCAG AA contrast ratio between foreground and background is < 4.5:1
Returns parsed dict of {key: hex_value} on success
contrast_ratio(fg: str, bg: str) -> float helper uses relative luminance formula
Tests pass: valid theme passes, missing key raises, low-contrast raises
ruff check passes

userStories.title: LLM client and prompt templates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a provider-agnostic LLM client and the system/user prompt templates for theme generation.
acceptanceCriteria: generator/prompt.py: build_prompt(sanitized_query: str) -> dict returns {system: str, user: str} using the prompt design from PRD §9.1
System prompt instructs: output ONLY key=value pairs, all 21 keys required, WCAG AA, dark default, ignore instructions in input
generator/llm.py: LLMClient class with generate(prompt: dict, provider) -> str method
generate() raises LLMError on HTTP error or empty response
generate() does NOT retry — callers handle retry
Tests pass: mock provider returns valid theme string, mock returns empty string raises LLMError
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Input sanitizer — full implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the real input sanitizer replacing the stub, with NFKC normalization and unicode safety.
acceptanceCriteria: sanitize_prompt() applies NFKC unicode normalization (unicodedata.normalize)
Strips leading/trailing whitespace, collapses internal whitespace to single spaces
Truncates to 200 UTF-8 characters (not code points) and issues a warning if truncated
Returns cleaned ASCII-safe string
Tests pass: emoji in input normalized, 250-char input truncated to 200, homoglyph normalized
ruff check passes

userStories.title: SQLite cache repository — embedding storage and lookup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the cache repository to store and retrieve embeddings as JSON arrays for the similarity search tier.
acceptanceCriteria: cache/db.py: save_theme() accepts optional embedding: list[float] parameter
Embedding stored as json.dumps(vector.tolist()) in the TEXT embedding column — use JSON serialization only, never binary object serialization
get_all_embeddings() -> list[tuple[int, list[float]]] returns (id, vector) for all rows with non-null embeddings
get_by_id(theme_id: int) -> dict returns full theme row
Tests pass: save with embedding, retrieve embedding, confirm JSON round-trip preserves values
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Prompt mode pipeline — full A1-A7 implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the complete prompt mode pipeline wiring sanitize → cache lookup → LLM generate → validate → cache.
acceptanceCriteria: modes/prompt_mode.py: generate_from_prompt(query: str, provider, target: str) -> str runs the full pipeline
Step A1-A2: sanitize and normalize query
Step A3: SHA256 exact cache lookup — returns cached theme_data string on hit
Step A4: placeholder (returns None — similarity search added in US-016/017)
Step A5: LLM generation with up to 3 retries on validation failure
Step A6: validate_theme() called; retry on failure
Step A7: save to cache with query_hash, input_type='prompt', provider, cost_usd
Returns serialized theme string in the requested target format
Tests pass: cache hit skips LLM, validation failure retries, max retries raises
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Typer CLI entry point — generate and config commands
userStories.description: As a user, I want a working CLI so I can run tty-theme generate from the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: cli/main.py: Typer app with 'generate' command accepting --prompt TEXT, --image PATH, --target TEXT (default: ghostty), --install flag, --name TEXT
'config' subapp with 'status' command printing provider, cache stats, spend to date
'seed' command that loads themes/index.json and inserts into SQLite cache
generate --prompt runs prompt_mode.generate_from_prompt() and prints output to stdout
Running 'tty-theme --help' shows all commands without error
pyproject.toml scripts entry: tty-theme = 'cli.main:app'
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Local provider adapters — Ollama, LM Studio, llamafile
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the three local model server adapters so users with Ollama/LM Studio/llamafile pay $0.
acceptanceCriteria: providers/ollama.py: OllamaProvider with generate() calling http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions
providers/lmstudio.py: LMStudioProvider calling http://localhost:1234/v1/chat/completions
providers/llamafile.py: LlamafileProvider calling http://localhost:8080/v1/chat/completions
All three use the same OpenAI-compatible request format
Each has a health_check() -> bool method that returns False (not raises) if server is not running
Base URL validated: must be http://localhost or http://127.0.0.1 — reject other hosts unless --allow-remote-llm flag
Tests pass: mock HTTP 200 returns content, mock connection error returns health_check False
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Cloud provider adapters — Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Groq, Mistral
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the five cloud LLM provider adapters so users can generate themes without a local model.
acceptanceCriteria: providers/anthropic.py, openai.py, gemini.py, groq.py, mistral.py each implement generate(prompt: dict, api_key: str) -> str
Each adapter uses the correct API endpoint and request format for its provider
Cheapest model hardcoded as default: claude-haiku-3-5, gpt-4o-mini, gemini-2.0-flash-lite, llama3-8b-8192, mistral-small
HTTP 429 raises RateLimitError (not retried in adapter — caller handles)
API key never logged — log statements scrub known key patterns before output
Tests pass: mock 200 returns content, mock 429 raises RateLimitError, missing key raises ConfigError
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Provider registry and auto-resolution chain
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the provider registry to auto-detect the best available provider in cost order.
acceptanceCriteria: providers/registry.py: resolve_provider(config: dict) -> Provider returns first available provider
Resolution order: --provider flag → GHOSTTY_PROVIDER env var → config file → auto-detect Ollama → LM Studio → llamafile → Gemini → Groq → Haiku → GPT-4o-mini → Mistral
Auto-detect calls health_check() on each local provider; first that returns True wins
Cloud provider selected only if a key is available via keychain or env var
Raises ConfigError with clear message if no provider is available
Tests pass: Ollama healthy → returns OllamaProvider, no providers available → raises ConfigError
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Keychain key management and config setup wizard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to store and retrieve API keys securely via the OS keychain and run a first-time setup wizard.
acceptanceCriteria: security/keystore.py: full implementation using keyring library — get_key(provider) reads from OS keychain, set_key(provider, key) writes to OS keychain
Keys never written to config.toml (hard error if attempted)
config.toml stored at ~/.config/tty-theme/config.toml with non-key fields only: provider, similarity_threshold, daily_spend_cap_usd, privacy_mode
'tty-theme config setup' interactive wizard prompts for provider choice and optional API key
'tty-theme config set-key <provider>' prompts for key with hidden input (typer.prompt secret=True)
'tty-theme config list' shows configured providers with masked keys (last 4 chars visible)
Tests pass: set_key then get_key returns same value, config.toml never contains key material
ruff check passes

userStories.title: MiniLM embeddings module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the local MiniLM embedding module to encode prompts as float vectors stored as JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: cache/embeddings.py: encode(text: str) -> list[float] uses sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model
Vectors stored via json.dumps(vector.tolist()) and loaded via json.loads() + numpy.array(..., dtype=numpy.float32) — JSON only, no binary serialization formats
cosine_similarity(a: list[float], b: list[float]) -> float computes dot product / (norm_a * norm_b)
Model loaded lazily (on first call) and cached in module-level variable
Tests pass: encode returns list of floats, cosine_similarity([1,0],[1,0])==1.0, cosine_similarity([1,0],[0,1])==0.0
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Tiered cache — similarity search tier (tier 2)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the similarity search tier so semantically similar prompts share cached results without an LLM call.
acceptanceCriteria: modes/prompt_mode.py: step A4 now encodes the query and checks cosine similarity against all cached embeddings
Returns the cached theme if best match score >= 0.85 (similarity_threshold from config)
Tier used logged: tier_used=2 in audit_log when similarity hit
New themes saved with their embedding (calls embeddings.encode() before cache.save_theme())
Tests pass: two semantically similar queries share a cache hit at threshold 0.85, dissimilar query misses
ruff check passes

userStories.title: SSRF guard — full implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the real SSRF guard replacing the stub, blocking RFC1918, loopback, and link-local addresses.
acceptanceCriteria: security/ssrf_guard.py: check_url(url: str) -> None raises ValueError for non-https:// URLs
Resolves hostname to IP; raises ValueError if IP is in RFC1918 (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16), loopback (127.0.0.0/8), link-local (169.254/16), IPv6 ::1
Timeout on DNS resolution: 5s
Raises ValueError for bare IP URLs that are private
Tests pass: https://example.com passes, http:// raises, 192.168.1.1 raises, 127.0.0.1 raises
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Safe image loader — magic bytes, size cap, EXIF strip
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the safe image loader so malicious or oversized images are rejected before processing.
acceptanceCriteria: image/loader.py: load_image(source: str) -> PIL.Image.Image accepts local path or HTTPS URL
For URLs: calls ssrf_guard.check_url() first, then fetches with 10s timeout, 10MB max download
Magic byte check: first 12 bytes must match PNG (89 50 4E 47), JPEG (FF D8 FF), WEBP (52 49 46 46 ... 57 45 42 50), or GIF (47 49 46 38) — raises ValueError otherwise
PIL.Image.MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS set to 4096*4096; DecompressionBombWarning promoted to error
Dimension cap: raises ValueError if width or height > 4096
EXIF stripped: image returned via PIL ImageOps.exif_transpose with .info cleared
For local paths: resolved to absolute path, must be within CWD or home directory
Tests pass: valid PNG loads, JPEG from URL loads, GIF raises ValueError, 5000px image raises
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Perceptual hash (pHash) computation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need perceptual hash computation so near-identical images share a cached theme.
acceptanceCriteria: image/phash.py: compute_phash(img: PIL.Image.Image) -> str using imagehash.phash(), returns hex string
hash_distance(h1: str, h2: str) -> int returns Hamming distance between two pHash hex strings
Tests pass: same image produces identical hash, slightly modified image has distance < 10, unrelated images have distance > 20
ruff check passes

userStories.title: k-means color extractor
userStories.description: As a developer, I need k-means color clustering to extract 16 dominant colors from an image.
acceptanceCriteria: image/extractor.py: extract_colors(img: PIL.Image.Image, k: int = 16) -> list[str] returns list of k hex color strings
Image resized to 200x200 before clustering (performance)
Uses sklearn.cluster.KMeans with k clusters on RGB pixels
Returns cluster centers as sorted hex strings (#RRGGBB)
Tests pass: solid red image returns a red-dominant palette, k=16 returns exactly 16 colors
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Palette mapper — image colors to Ghostty keys
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the palette mapper to assign extracted image colors to the 21 Ghostty theme keys.
acceptanceCriteria: image/mapper.py: map_palette(colors: list[str]) -> dict maps 16 extracted colors to Ghostty keys
Darkest color (lowest luminance) → background
Lightest color (highest luminance) → foreground
Highest-saturation colors → palette 1-6 (ANSI colors)
Remaining colors fill palette 0, 7-15 by luminance order
cursor-color = palette 3 (yellow-ish), selection-background = palette 4 (blue-ish), selection-foreground = background
After mapping: validate WCAG AA contrast ratio (fg/bg >= 4.5:1); if fails, lighten foreground by incrementing luminance until passing
Tests pass: all-dark image produces mapping where background is darker than foreground, contrast passes
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Image mode pipeline — full B1-B8 implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the complete image mode pipeline so users can generate themes from photos.
acceptanceCriteria: modes/image_mode.py: generate_from_image(source: str, target: str, refine: bool = False) -> str runs the full pipeline
B1: validate source (local magic-byte or URL SSRF guard)
B2: load_image() with size/dim caps
B3: compute_phash()
B4: pHash cache lookup — returns cached theme on hit
B5: extract_colors(k=16)
B6: map_palette() → Ghostty dict
B7: if refine=True, calls LLM with image refinement prompt (PRD §9.2) and validates output
B8: validate_theme(), cache by pHash with input_type='image', serialize and return
CLI: 'tty-theme generate --image ./photo.jpg' works end-to-end
Tests pass: valid image produces 21-key theme, pHash cache hit skips extraction
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Pre-seeded community theme index
userStories.description: As a user, I want 50 bundled themes available immediately without an API key or local model.
acceptanceCriteria: themes/index.json contains at least 50 theme entries, each with: name, source ('community'), license ('MIT' or 'CC0'), author, theme_data (Ghostty key=value string), palette_json (16 hex colors)
Every theme_data passes validate_theme()
Themes cover diverse aesthetics: dark, light, neon, pastel, earth tones, high contrast
'tty-theme seed' command inserts all entries into SQLite with source='community'
Seed is idempotent (duplicate query_hash skipped, not error)
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Daily spend cap and cost circuit breaker
userStories.description: As a user, I want a daily spend cap so I never accidentally spend more than my configured limit on LLM API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: cache/db.py: log_cost(provider: str, cost_usd: float) writes to cost_log; get_daily_spend() -> float sums today's cost_usd
modes/prompt_mode.py: checks get_daily_spend() before LLM call; raises SpendCapError if >= daily_spend_cap_usd (default $1.00)
LLM HTTP 429: exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s), max 3 retries, then raises RateLimitError — no infinite loops
'tty-theme config status' prints today's spend and remaining cap
Tests pass: spend at cap raises SpendCapError, spend below cap proceeds
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Rate limiting module — token bucket for CLI and API mode
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a token bucket rate limiter for future API/web mode integration.
acceptanceCriteria: security/rate_limiter.py: TokenBucket class with capacity, refill_rate (tokens/second), consume() -> bool
consume() returns True if a token is available and deducts it; returns False if bucket is empty
RateLimiter(ip_hash: str) wraps TokenBucket; configured for 10 req/min, 50 req/hour per IP
State stored in SQLite rate_limits table (ip_hash, tokens, last_refill)
Tests pass: 11 rapid consume() calls returns False on 11th, after 6s refill returns True
ruff check passes

userStories.title: --install flag for Ghostty and iTerm2
userStories.description: As a user, I want to install generated themes directly to my terminal's config directory with a single flag.
acceptanceCriteria: generate --install --name <name> writes the theme file to the correct path for the target
Ghostty: ~/.config/ghostty/themes/<name> (creates parent dirs if needed)
iTerm2: ~/Library/Application Support/iTerm2/DynamicProfiles/<name>.itermcolors
Prints confirmation: 'Theme installed to <path>'
Raises clear error if install path is not writable
'tty-theme search <query>' command queries SQLite and prints matching theme names
'tty-theme list' prints all cached theme names
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Local GCP emulator environment — docker-compose.yml and Firebase emulator config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a fully local GCP-equivalent stack so I can develop and test the full Phase 4-7 feature set without touching real GCP infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml in project root: 'api' service (builds from Dockerfile), 'firebase-emulator' service (firebase/firebase-tools image), port bindings: api=8000, firestore-emu=8080, auth-emu=9099, hosting-emu=5000, emulator-ui=4000
firebase.json: emulators block enables auth (9099), firestore (8080), hosting (5000), ui (4000); export/import dir set to .firebase-data/ for persistence between restarts

.env.example: FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST=localhost:8080, FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST=localhost:9099, FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=tty-theme-local, GEMINI_API_KEY=, GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=, GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=, ENVIRONMENT=development
.gitignore updated: .env, .firebase-data/, .firebase/
docker compose up brings up all services; 'curl localhost:8000/health' returns {status: 'ok'}
DEVELOPMENT.md documents: (1) prerequisites (Docker, Node, firebase-tools), (2) 'cp .env.example .env && docker compose up', (3) emulator UI at http://localhost:4000, (4) how to seed test data
ruff check passes
userStories.notes: LOCAL-ONLY: No gcloud or firebase deploy commands. All GCP services emulated locally via Firebase Emulator Suite + Docker Compose.

userStories.title: FastAPI app skeleton — generate endpoint and health check
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the FastAPI app with a /generate endpoint so the CLI pipeline is accessible over HTTP.
acceptanceCriteria: api/main.py: FastAPI app with GET /health returning {status: 'ok'}
POST /v1/generate accepts JSON body: {prompt: str, target: str = 'ghostty'} or {image_url: str, target: str, refine: bool = false}
Calls prompt_mode.generate_from_prompt() or image_mode.generate_from_image() and returns {theme: str, provider: str, tier_used: int}
CORS configured: allow origins=['https://tty-theme.dev'] in production, ['*'] in development
Returns HTTP 422 on validation error with clear message
Returns HTTP 429 on rate limit or spend cap exceeded
Tests pass (httpx AsyncClient): /health returns 200, POST /v1/generate with mock provider returns theme
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Firestore backend adapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Firestore-backed ThemeRepository so the production API uses GCP instead of SQLite.
acceptanceCriteria: cache/firestore_db.py: FirestoreThemeRepository implements the same interface as ThemeRepository (init_db, save_theme, get_by_hash, list_themes, get_all_embeddings, get_by_id, log_cost, get_daily_spend)
Uses google-cloud-firestore; themes collection stores same fields as SQLite schema
Embeddings stored as Firestore vector field (list of floats) for FindNearest support
Repository selected at runtime: if FIRESTORE_PROJECT env var set AND ENVIRONMENT != 'development' -> FirestoreThemeRepository, else SQLiteThemeRepository
Local testing uses Firebase Firestore Emulator: FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST=localhost:8080 env var; google-cloud-firestore SDK auto-routes to emulator when this var is set — no code changes needed
Tests pass using Firestore Emulator (FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST=localhost:8080 set in pytest env): save_theme and get_by_hash round-trip correctly
ruff check passes
userStories.notes: LOCAL: Tests run against Firebase Firestore Emulator, not real GCP.

userStories.title: Dockerfile and Cloud Run deploy configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a production-ready Dockerfile and Cloud Run config so the API deploys to GCP.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile in project root: Python 3.11-slim base, uv sync --no-dev, uvicorn api/main.py on port 8000, non-root user (uid 1000)

docker-compose.yml updated: 'api' service builds from Dockerfile, mounts .env, depends_on firebase-emulator, sets FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST=firebase-emulator:8080 and FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST=firebase-emulator:9099
.dockerignore: .env, *.pyc, __pycache__, .git, tests/, .firebase-data/
'docker compose build && docker compose up api' starts the API; 'curl localhost:8000/health' returns 200

DEPLOY.md documents the future Cloud Run deploy command for reference (not executed): gcloud run deploy tty-theme-api --region=us-central1 --project=tinkerwithtech-214914 --min-instances=0 --max-instances=10 --memory=256Mi --concurrency=80 --set-secrets=GEMINI_API_KEY=...
cloudbuild.yaml stub: steps run pip-audit, bandit, ruff, pytest — intentionally omits 'gcloud run deploy' step (placeholder comment: '# TODO: uncomment for production deploy')
ruff check passes
userStories.notes: LOCAL-ONLY: No gcloud run deploy. Cloud Run deploy documented in DEPLOY.md for future use only.

userStories.title: API rate limiting and audit log middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I need per-IP rate limiting and an audit log in the API so abuse is tracked and throttled.
acceptanceCriteria: api/middleware.py: RateLimitMiddleware reads X-Forwarded-For, hashes IP with SHA256, checks TokenBucket
Limits: 10 req/min, 50 req/hour per IP hash — returns HTTP 429 with Retry-After header
AuditLogMiddleware writes to audit_log (ip_hash, query_hash, input_type, provider, tier_used, cost_usd, status) after each /v1/generate response
Raw IP never logged — only SHA256(IP)
Spend circuit breaker: if hourly Firestore cost_log sum > $10 (configurable), /v1/generate returns 503 with {error: 'service_temporarily_unavailable', reason: 'spend_limit'}
Tests pass: 11th request from same IP returns 429, audit_log entry written for successful request
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Vite + Tailwind static site scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the frontend scaffold with design tokens, Space Mono font, and Tailwind config so UI components have a consistent base.
acceptanceCriteria: web/ directory with index.html, vite.config.js, package.json (vite, tailwindcss)
tailwind.config.js extends theme with design tokens from PRD §19.1: background #0F172A, surface #1E293B, border #334155, muted #64748B, foreground #F8FAFC, accent #22C55E, ai #818CF8
Space Mono loaded from Google Fonts in index.html <head>
Global CSS: body uses background and foreground tokens, font-family Space Mono
Ko-fi support link in header nav: URL https://ko-fi.com/Y8Y51KQK8E, styled per PRD §19.8, opens in new tab
GA4 placeholder comment in index.html (no real tag ID hardcoded)
'npm run build' completes without errors
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: page loads, Space Mono renders, dark background visible

userStories.title: Home page — provider selector, target selector, prompt/image tabs
userStories.description: As a user, I want the generator home page with provider and terminal selectors and a prompt input so I can generate themes from the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: / route: ProviderSelector pill group (Gemini default, BYOK expand inline per PRD §6.4)
TargetSelector pill toggle: Ghostty / iTerm2
Prompt tab: textarea with 200-char counter (aria-live), suggestion chip buttons below
Image tab: file picker + optional HTTPS URL input
Submit button labeled 'Generate' with #22C55E accent
POST to /v1/generate API on submit; loading spinner while waiting
On success: navigate to results page with theme data
BYOK key entry: shows security disclaimer from PRD §6.4, stored in localStorage tty-theme:byok:<provider>
prefers-reduced-motion: all transitions 0.01ms
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: type a prompt, click Generate, see loading state

userStories.title: HeroTerminal animation component
userStories.description: As a user, I want the animated terminal preview hero so the product's value is immediately visible on page load.
acceptanceCriteria: HeroTerminal component: macOS traffic lights (red #FF5F57, yellow #FFBD2E, green #28CA41) in window chrome
Container has perspective 1200px, rotateX(4deg) rotateY(-2deg) 3D tilt
Float animation: oscillates rotateX(2-4deg) rotateY(-2..2deg) over 6s ease-in-out loop
Scanline overlay: repeating-linear-gradient every 4px, 4% opacity, pointer-events none
TypeWriter: JS char-by-char at 45ms/char, outputs demo theme cycle (cyberpunk neon rain -> tokyo midnight -> aurora borealis ice)
PaletteStrip: 16-color strip at bottom, swaps with 400ms transition-colors
prefers-reduced-motion collapses all animations to instant
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: hero loads, typing animation runs, palette strip updates on theme change

userStories.title: Results page — palette grid, format toggle, copy/install/share
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my generated theme with color swatches, copy the config, and share it.
acceptanceCriteria: /t/:slug/preview route: PaletteGrid 8x2 swatch grid (normal row palette 0-7, bright row palette 8-15), hover shows hex + ANSI index
FormatToggle: Ghostty / iTerm2 switch — updates displayed config text
Config displayed in <pre> block with syntax colors
Copy button: copies config to clipboard, shows 'Copied!' for 2s (aria-live='polite')
Install button: copies 'tty-theme install <slug>' to clipboard
Share button: copies tty-theme.dev/t/<slug> to clipboard, shows ShareToast 2.5s auto-dismiss
Similar themes section: shows 3 ThemeCard components
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: both format toggles show valid config, copy button works

userStories.title: Firebase Hosting deploy config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Firebase Hosting configured so the web UI deploys to tty-theme.dev with CDN and HTTPS.
acceptanceCriteria: firebase.json hosting block: public=web/dist, rewrites /api/* → http://localhost:8000 (local dev), headers: HSTS max-age=31536000, X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff
.firebaserc: project alias 'default' = 'tty-theme-local' (local emulator project ID)
DEVELOPMENT.md updated: run 'npm run build' in web/, then 'firebase emulators:start --only hosting' serves built site at http://localhost:5000
firebase.json emulators block already set in US-028 — verify hosting emulator entry is present at port 5000
GA4 placeholder comment in index.html only — no real tag ID, no firebase.json environment substitution yet (deferred to production deploy)
DEPLOY.md documents: 'firebase deploy --only hosting' for future production deploy to tty-theme.dev
ruff check passes
userStories.notes: LOCAL-ONLY: Uses Firebase Hosting Emulator (port 5000). No 'firebase deploy' run.

userStories.title: Firebase Auth — GitHub OAuth login/logout
userStories.description: As a user, I want to log in with GitHub so I can publish themes to the community gallery.
acceptanceCriteria: Firebase Auth initialized in web app with GitHub OAuth provider AND Firebase Auth Emulator support: initializeApp() followed by connectAuthEmulator(auth, 'http://localhost:9099') when VITE_USE_EMULATOR=true
Login button in header nav: 'Sign in with GitHub' opens popup, sets currentUser on success
Logout button replaces login when signed in, shows GitHub avatar + username
auth.currentUser.getIdToken() used for authenticated API requests (Authorization: Bearer <token>)
API middleware verifies Firebase ID token; when FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST env var is set, uses emulator for token verification (firebase-admin SDK auto-routes to emulator)
DEVELOPMENT.md updated: documents creating a test user in emulator UI at http://localhost:4000/auth
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: with emulator running, login flow completes using a test account created in emulator UI
userStories.notes: LOCAL: Uses Firebase Auth Emulator (port 9099). No real GitHub OAuth credentials needed for local testing — emulator bypasses OAuth.

userStories.title: Community gallery API — list, detail, download endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the read-only gallery API so the community browse page can fetch themes.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /v1/themes: returns paginated list, query params: sort (downloads|likes|newest), target (ghostty|iterm2), q (full-text search), page, limit (default 20, max 100)
GET /v1/themes/:slug: returns full theme detail including ghostty_data, iterm2_data, palette_json, author, stats
GET /v1/themes/:slug/download/:fmt: returns theme file with correct Content-Type and Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=<slug>.<ext>
download increments download_count atomically in Firestore
Cache-Control: public, max-age=300 on list/detail endpoints
Tests pass: list returns 200 with items array, unknown slug returns 404, download increments counter
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Gallery write API — publish, like, flag endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the write endpoints so authenticated users can publish themes and anyone can like or flag.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /v1/themes: requires Firebase auth token, accepts {slug, display_name, ghostty_data?, iterm2_data?, palette_json, source, tags[]}
Server validates: slug matches [a-z0-9-]+ (3-60 chars), slug not in blocklist of reserved words, palette_json is 16 valid hex colors, ghostty_data and iterm2_data (if provided) pass validate_theme()
Rate limit: max 10 publishes/day per user
POST /v1/themes/:slug/like: fingerprint = SHA256(IP+UA), one like per fingerprint, increments like_count
DELETE /v1/themes/:slug/like: removes like
POST /v1/themes/:slug/flag: sets is_flagged=True after 3 flags
DELETE /v1/themes/:slug: auth required, author only
Tests pass: publish with valid data creates theme, duplicate slug returns 409, invalid palette_json returns 422
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Community gallery browse page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse community themes with sorting, filtering, and search so I can discover themes I like.
acceptanceCriteria: /browse route: SortBar with Downloads / Likes / Newest tabs, terminal filter (All / Ghostty / iTerm2), search input
ThemeCard grid: each card shows 8-color strip, name, author, target badges, download count, like count
Clicking ThemeCard navigates to /t/:slug
Search input debounced 300ms, updates results without page reload
Pagination: 'Load more' button appends next page
Filter state persisted in URL query params
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: sort tabs switch ordering, search filters results, load more appends cards

userStories.title: Theme detail page — shareable URL and og:image
userStories.description: As a user, I want a shareable theme page at /t/:slug with a social preview image so I can share themes on social media.
acceptanceCriteria: /t/:slug route: full theme detail — 16-swatch PaletteGrid, FormatToggle, config display, copy/download/install/share buttons
'Clone and remix' button pre-fills generator with the theme's inspiration prompt
og:title, og:description, og:image meta tags generated server-side with palette strip PNG (16 swatches, 480x60px)
og:image served by GET /v1/themes/:slug/og-image endpoint returning PNG
Install command shown: 'tty-theme install <slug>'
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: page loads with correct theme colors, share button copies URL, download button downloads file

userStories.title: CLI community gallery commands
userStories.description: As a user, I want CLI commands to publish, browse, install, and share gallery themes.
acceptanceCriteria: 'tty-theme publish <name> --target ghostty --target iterm2' looks up theme in local cache and POSTs to /v1/themes (requires GitHub auth token in keychain)
'tty-theme browse --sort downloads' prints a table of top themes with name, author, downloads
'tty-theme install <slug> --target ghostty' calls GET /v1/themes/:slug/download/ghostty and writes to Ghostty install path
'tty-theme share <name>' prints https://tty-theme.dev/t/<slug> and copies to clipboard
'tty-theme auth login' opens browser for GitHub OAuth, stores token in keychain
Tests pass: install command writes correct file to Ghostty path, browse command prints table
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Security hardening — bandit, pip-audit, ruff S-rules in CI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all security gates enforced in CI so no unsafe code patterns slip through.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml [tool.bandit] excludes tests/, configures S-rules: S301, S302, S303, S307, S324
pyproject.toml [tool.ruff.lint] selects S rules; S101 skipped in tests/, S603/S607 allowed for subprocess with shell=False
cloudbuild.yaml runs 'pip-audit --require-hashes' and fails build on any known CVE
pre-commit config (.pre-commit-config.yaml): detect-secrets, ruff, bandit hooks
.gitignore: .env, *.key, *.pem, config.toml
No eval, exec, or unsafe deserialization present in non-test source code
ruff check passes, bandit passes

userStories.title: GCP IAM least privilege and Secret Manager wiring
userStories.description: As a developer, I need GCP IAM configured with least privilege and all secrets in Secret Manager.
acceptanceCriteria: security/secrets.py: get_secret(name: str) -> str — reads from Secret Manager in production (google-cloud-secret-manager), falls back to os.environ[name] when ENVIRONMENT=development or GCP_PROJECT not set
api/main.py updated to use get_secret() for GEMINI_API_KEY, GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET at startup
All local dev: secrets provided via .env file (loaded by python-dotenv in development mode only); .env never committed
.env.example has all secret keys with empty values and a comment warning not to commit .env
DEPLOY.md documents: service account tty-theme-api needs only roles/datastore.user, roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor, roles/monitoring.metricWriter; no roles/owner or roles/editor
DEPLOY.md documents gcloud commands to create secrets: 'gcloud secrets create GEMINI_API_KEY --data-file=-' (not executed by Ralph)
ruff check passes
userStories.notes: LOCAL: Secrets come from .env file. Secret Manager integration wired but not tested against real GCP — tested via env var fallback.

userStories.title: README and CLI documentation
userStories.description: As a user, I want a comprehensive README so I can install and use tty-theme without reading the source code.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md: project overview, installation (pip install tty-theme / uv tool install tty-theme), quickstart examples
Sections: Prompt mode, Image mode, Configuration (providers + keys), Output formats, CLI reference table
Provider setup guide: how to run Ollama, how to set a cloud API key
Security section: links to SECURITY.md (vulnerability reporting)
Contributing section: link to CONTRIBUTING.md, dev setup commands
Badges: PyPI version, CI status, license (MIT)

userStories.title: PyPI publish, SBOM, and sigstore signing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the release pipeline so tty-theme is installable via pip with a verifiable supply chain.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml: [build-system] requires hatchling, [project] includes version, license=MIT, classifiers, entry-points
cloudbuild.yaml: on git tag push, runs 'python -m build', 'cyclonedx-py bom . -o sbom.json', 'sigstore sign dist/*.whl dist/*.tar.gz'
'uv build' produces .whl and .tar.gz in dist/
GitHub Release: sbom.json and .sig files attached as release assets
PyPI publish step uses trusted publishing (OIDC) — no PyPI token stored in secrets
ruff check passes

userStories.title: Monitoring alerts and domain setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Cloud Monitoring alerts and the tty-theme.dev domain wired up so I know when the service is degraded.
acceptanceCriteria: DEPLOY.md documents (but does NOT execute) all Cloud Monitoring alert policies: p99 latency > 5s, error rate > 5% over 5 min, Gemini cost > $5/day
DEPLOY.md documents custom domain setup: firebase.json hosting.site target + 'firebase hosting:channel:deploy' workflow
api/main.py: /metrics endpoint returns Prometheus-format text (request count, latency histogram, error count) using prometheus-client library — for local monitoring without GCP
docker-compose.yml updated: optional 'prometheus' service (prom/prometheus image) with prometheus.yml scraping localhost:8000/metrics at 15s interval
prometheus.yml created in project root with static_configs targeting api:8000
DEVELOPMENT.md updated: 'docker compose --profile monitoring up' starts Prometheus at http://localhost:9090
ruff check passes
userStories.notes: LOCAL: No GCP Monitoring deployed. Prometheus local stack for development observability. GCP alerts documented in DEPLOY.md for future use.

userStories.title: Terraform root module — providers, backend, variables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Terraform root module so all GCP resources are defined as code and can be validated locally without applying.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/ directory with: main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf, terraform.tfvars.example
versions.tf: terraform required_version >=1.6; required_providers google>=5.0, google-beta>=5.0
variables.tf: project_id, region (default us-central1), environment (default development), app_name (default tty-theme)
main.tf: google provider configured with project=var.project_id, region=var.region
GCS backend block in main.tf with bucket=tty-theme-tfstate, prefix=terraform/state (backend=false for local validate)
terraform.tfvars.example: project_id=tinkerwithtech-214914, region=us-central1, environment=production
'terraform init -backend=false && terraform validate' passes with no errors
README section in DEPLOY.md: 'cd terraform && terraform init && terraform plan -var-file=terraform.tfvars'
userStories.notes: LOCAL: Run 'terraform init -backend=false && terraform validate && terraform plan' — no apply.

userStories.title: Terraform — Artifact Registry and Cloud Build IAM
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Artifact Registry provisioned so Docker images have a home and Cloud Build can push to it.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/modules/artifact_registry/main.tf: google_artifact_registry_repository resource, format=DOCKER, location=var.region, repository_id=tty-theme-api
terraform/modules/artifact_registry/variables.tf: project_id, region, repository_id
terraform/modules/artifact_registry/outputs.tf: repository_url output (used by Cloud Run module)
Cloud Build SA binding: roles/artifactregistry.writer granted to cloudbuild SA
Module called from root main.tf
'terraform validate' passes

userStories.title: Terraform — IAM service account (least privilege)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the tty-theme-api service account with only the roles it needs.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/modules/iam/main.tf: google_service_account resource name=tty-theme-api
IAM bindings: roles/datastore.user, roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor, roles/monitoring.metricWriter — nothing broader
terraform/modules/iam/outputs.tf: service_account_email output
Module called from root main.tf
'terraform validate' passes

userStories.title: Terraform — Secret Manager (secret names, no values)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Secret Manager secrets declared in Terraform so names are consistent between IaC and application code.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/modules/secret_manager/main.tf: google_secret_manager_secret resources for GEMINI_API_KEY, GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET
replication.auto{} block on each secret (not manual, for simplicity)
Secret *versions* (actual values) are NOT in Terraform — values set manually via gcloud or CI
terraform/modules/secret_manager/outputs.tf: secret_ids map output
Module called from root main.tf
'terraform validate' passes
userStories.notes: Values are never in Terraform state. Only the secret resource shells are managed.

userStories.title: Terraform — Firestore database
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Firestore provisioned so the production database exists before the API starts.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/modules/firestore/main.tf: google_firestore_database resource, type=FIRESTORE_NATIVE, location_id=nam5
terraform/modules/firestore/variables.tf: project_id, location_id
google_project_service to enable firestore.googleapis.com
Module called from root main.tf
'terraform validate' passes

userStories.title: Terraform — Cloud Run service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Cloud Run service defined in Terraform so the API deployment is reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/modules/cloud_run/main.tf: google_cloud_run_v2_service resource
Container image: var.image_url (populated from Artifact Registry output)
Env vars: ENVIRONMENT=production, GCP_PROJECT=var.project_id, FIRESTORE_PROJECT=var.project_id
Secret env vars via volumes: GEMINI_API_KEY, GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET from Secret Manager
scaling: min_instance_count=0, max_instance_count=10; resources: limits cpu=1, memory=256Mi
service_account from IAM module output
google_cloud_run_v2_service_iam_member: allUsers gets roles/run.invoker (public API)
terraform/modules/cloud_run/outputs.tf: service_url output
Module called from root main.tf, depends_on iam and secret_manager modules
'terraform validate' passes

userStories.description: As a developer, I need Cloud Monitoring alert policies in Terraform so SLOs are enforced from day one.

google_monitoring_notification_channel resource: email type, var.alert_email
terraform/modules/monitoring/variables.tf: project_id, alert_email, service_name

userStories.title: Terraform — local validate CI gate + terraform.tfvars.example
userStories.description: As a developer, I need terraform validate to run in CI and a complete tfvars example so onboarding is frictionless.
acceptanceCriteria: cloudbuild.yaml updated: new step runs 'terraform init -backend=false && terraform validate' before Docker build step
terraform.tfvars.example: all required variables with comments, no secret values
terraform/README.md: prerequisites (Terraform 1.6+, gcloud auth), commands (init, plan, apply), module dependency diagram
All terraform files pass 'terraform fmt -check'
'terraform init -backend=false && terraform validate && terraform plan -var-file=terraform.tfvars.example -var="project_id=placeholder"' exits 0
userStories.notes: LOCAL: CI runs validate+plan only. 'terraform apply' is a manual production step documented in DEPLOY.md.
